title: Pretix
description: Open-source event ticketing and registration platform for selling tickets to conferences, festivals and exhibitions (Eventbrite alternative).
installation: Pretix is a Django stack. This template runs the PRIMARY web container (image pretix/standalone with command 'web', listening on container port 80 - nginx+gunicorn under supervisord), wired to the Postgres and Valkey/Redis add-ons provisioned here. The image is configured via PRETIX_<SECTION>_<KEY> environment variables (the env-var equivalent of /etc/pretix/pretix.cfg). You MUST deploy the companion services as separate apps, all sharing the SAME Postgres, Redis and env keys below: (1) Task worker - image pretix/standalone with command 'taskworker' (Celery; REQUIRED - sending emails, generating exports/invoices and all async jobs fail without it); (2) Cron/beat - image pretix/standalone with command 'cron' (periodic/scheduled tasks). Set PRETIX_PRETIX_URL to this app's public URL (token below). ROTATE before exposing: PRETIX_DJANGO_SECRET (via 'openssl rand -hex 50'), the Postgres add-on password, and configure real SMTP (PRETIX_MAIL_* are placeholders - pretix cannot send tickets without working email). First run: the web container applies DB migrations on boot; create the initial admin with 'pretix createsuperuser' (or set PRETIX_PRETIXD_* and use the web onboarding). The /data volume holds uploaded media, the data secret and the cache - mount the SAME volume (RWX) on the worker if you generate exports there. Redis here is used for cache, sessions and as the Celery broker. Docs: https://docs.pretix.eu/self-hosting/installation/docker_smallscale/
